L1.area: HTTP request handling and remote communication utilities

L2.description: An improper error handling in HTTPS request management allows remote attackers able to intercept the communication to remotely disable certificate verification on the client side. When a TLS handshake error occurs, the application automatically retries the request with certificate validation disabled, without requiring explicit user consent. This enables man-in-the-middle attacks, including the ability to impersonate update servers and push malicious updates.

L3.area: HTTPS request utility functions and their TLS error recovery handling
L3.description: An improper error handling in HTTPS request management allows remote attackers to remotely disable certificate verification when a TLS handshake error occurs during communication with remote update and package servers. The application automatically retries the failed request with certificate validation disabled, without requiring an explicit insecure flag from the caller, enabling man-in-the-middle attacks including the ability to push malicious updates.

ground_truth.reason: The http_request() utility function automatically disables TLS certificate verification and retries the request when a TLS handshake error occurs, allowing man-in-the-middle attackers to intercept and modify HTTPS communications including update payloads.
